title: Dutch A2 Fill-in-the-Blank Exercises – 6 Texts × 25 Questions

exercises.sentence: Sociale media zijn ___ de hele Nederlandse bevolking populair.

hint.en: Social media are popular among the entire Dutch population.
hint.explanation: 'Populair bij' = popular with/among a group. Fixed prepositional collocation. 'Populair voor' = popular for a purpose; 'in/aan' don't collocate with 'populair'.

exercises.sentence: Dat doen ze ___ om contact te maken.

hint.en: They do that mainly to make contact.
hint.explanation: 'Vooral' = mainly/above all. It expresses the primary motivation. 'Altijd' = always; 'soms' = sometimes — neither captures 'above all'.

exercises.sentence: Zestig procent van de gebruikers heeft ___ sociale media meer contact met vrienden.

hint.en: Sixty percent of users have more contact with friends thanks to social media.
hint.explanation: 'Dankzij' = thanks to (positive cause). 'Door' = because of (neutral/negative). 'Via' = via (channel, not cause). 'Met' = with.

exercises.sentence: ___ gebruiken ze sociale media om nieuws te volgen.

hint.en: In addition, they use social media to follow the news.
hint.explanation: 'Daarnaast' = in addition/besides. It adds a second point. 'Daarvoor' = for that/before that; 'daarna' = after that; 'daarmee' = with that.

exercises.sentence: De meeste gebruikers besteden ___ dan een uur per dag aan sociale media.

hint.en: Most users spend less than an hour per day on social media.
hint.explanation: 'Minder dan' = less than. The text states most users are under one hour. 'Meer dan' = more than (contradicts the statistic). 'Weinig' doesn't pair with 'dan'.

exercises.sentence: Het ___ echter op dat jongeren tussen de 18 en 25 jaar veel vaker op sociale media zitten.

hint.en: However, it is noticeable that young people between 18 and 25 are on social media much more often.
hint.explanation: 'Het valt op dat' = it is noticeable that. Fixed Dutch expression. 'Het komt op/staat op/gaat op' are all wrong idioms for this meaning.

exercises.sentence: Er ___ ook een toename van het aantal ouderen op sociale media.

hint.en: There is also an increase in the number of elderly people on social media.
hint.explanation: 'Er is' + singular noun 'een toename'. 'Er zijn' is for plural nouns. 'Er heeft/wordt' are wrong existential constructions.

exercises.sentence: Ze zijn handiger met hun smartphone of computer ___.

hint.en: They have become more skilled with their smartphone or computer.
hint.explanation: 'Worden' = to become. Perfect tense uses 'zijn' + 'geworden'. This expresses a change of state. 'Geweest' = been (been → no change); 'gegaan' = gone.

exercises.sentence: Ze communiceren ___ sociale media met hun kleinkinderen.

hint.en: They communicate with their grandchildren via social media.
hint.explanation: 'Via' = via/through (a channel or medium). 'Communiceren via' is the standard collocation. 'Door sociale media' = through/caused by social media (different nuance).

exercises.sentence: De meeste gebruikers delen foto's van ___ gebeurtenissen, zoals een huwelijk.

hint.en: Most users share photos of important events, such as a wedding.
hint.explanation: Adjective before a de-word plural ('gebeurtenissen') takes -e: 'belangrijke'. 'Belangrijk' is predicative (no -e). 'Belangrikste' = most important (superlative).

exercises.sentence: Ze plaatsen ___ een paar keer per jaar een bericht op hun profiel.

hint.en: They post only a few times a year on their profile.
hint.explanation: 'Slechts' = only/merely (formal, written register). 'Alleen/maar/enkel' are synonyms but the text uses 'slechts', which matches the informational register.

exercises.sentence: Uit onderzoek ___ dat steeds meer jongeren aan sociale media verslaafd zijn.

hint.en: Research shows that increasingly more young people are addicted to social media.
hint.explanation: 'Uit onderzoek blijkt dat' = research reveals/shows that. Fixed academic Dutch phrase. 'Blijkt' is the specific verb that collocates with 'uit onderzoek'.

exercises.sentence: Via hun smartphone zijn ze eigenlijk ___ met sociale media bezig.

hint.en: Via their smartphone they are practically constantly busy with social media.
hint.explanation: 'Constant' = constantly (adverb). All options mean 'always/constantly' but 'constant' is the text's exact word. At A2, recognising this colloquial adverb is important.

exercises.sentence: Deze jongeren ___ stress als ze hun berichten niet kunnen lezen.

hint.en: These young people get stressed when they cannot read their messages.
hint.explanation: 'Stress krijgen' = to get/become stressed. 'Krijgen' expresses acquiring/developing a state. 'Hebben stress' = to have stress (static state already possessed).

exercises.sentence: Ze kunnen ook ___ krijgen.

hint.en: They can also get sleep problems.
hint.explanation: 'Slaapproblemen' = sleep problems (compound noun, plural). Dutch compounds are always written as one word. 'Slaap problemen' (two words) is a spelling error.

exercises.sentence: Ze halen soms ___ studieresultaten.

hint.en: They sometimes get worse study results.
hint.explanation: 'Slechtere' = worse (comparative attributive before plural noun). Formula: 'slecht' + comparative '-er' + adjective ending '-e' = 'slechtere'. 'Slechte' = bad (not comparative).

exercises.sentence: Ze vinden het eigenlijk ___ van hun tijd om sociale media te gebruiken.

hint.en: They actually find it a waste of their time to use social media.
hint.explanation: 'Zonde van de tijd' = a waste of time. Fixed Dutch expression. 'Jammer' = a pity. 'Slecht/erg' describe quality or degree, not waste of time.

exercises.sentence: Ook vinden ze de ___ via sociale media irritant.

hint.en: They also find the advertisements via social media annoying.
hint.explanation: 'Advertenties' = advertisements (the text's word). 'Reclames' = commercials (radio/TV). 'Berichten' = messages. 'Posts' is English.

exercises.sentence: Daarnaast zijn ze ___ over hun privacy.

hint.en: In addition, they are worried about their privacy.
hint.explanation: 'Bezorgd over' = worried/concerned about. Fixed preposition 'over'. 'Bang voor' = afraid of. 'Ongerust over' also works, but 'bezorgd' is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Een deel van de gebruikers zoekt op sociale media ___ evenementen.

hint.en: Part of the users looks for events on social media.
hint.explanation: 'Zoeken naar' = to look for/search for. Fixed Dutch verb-preposition combination. 'Zoeken voor' = to look for (on behalf of someone else). 'Naar' is the correct preposition here.

exercises.sentence: Sociale media zijn ___ de hele Nederlandse bevolking populair.

hint.en: Social media are popular with the entire Dutch population.
hint.explanation: 'Populair bij' = popular with/among. 'Onder' = among (also possible in some contexts but 'bij' is the text's choice and more common at A2 level).

exercises.sentence: De helft van hen ___ één tot drie uur per dag aan sociale media.

hint.en: Half of them spend one to three hours per day on social media.
hint.explanation: 'Besteden' = to spend (time). 'De helft' is a singular collective noun → third person singular: 'besteedt' (with -t). 'Besteden' is plural. 'Doet' = does (wrong verb).

exercises.sentence: Het gebruik van sociale media heeft een aantal ___, vooral bij jongeren.

hint.en: The use of social media has a number of risks, especially among young people.
hint.explanation: Dutch words ending in a vowel (except -e) form their plural with apostrophe-s: risico → risico's. 'Risicos' (no apostrophe) is a spelling error. 'Risici' is Latin/archaic.

exercises.sentence: Zij delen ook foto's van ___ activiteiten, zoals een bezoek aan de sportschool.

hint.en: They also share photos of daily activities, such as a visit to the gym.
hint.explanation: 'Dagelijkse activiteiten' = daily activities. Adjective before de-word plural takes -e: 'dagelijkse'. 'Dagelijks' is the uninflected adverb/predicate form.

exercises.sentence: Tot slot willen mensen ___ sociale media volgen wat andere mensen doen.

hint.en: Finally, people want to follow what other people do via social media.
hint.explanation: 'Via sociale media' = through/via social media (channel). 'Op sociale media' = on social media (location). Both are used, but 'via' here emphasises the medium/channel, which is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Ik heb vegetarische pasta ___, ik hoop dat het lekker is.

hint.en: I made vegetarian pasta, I hope it's good.
hint.explanation: 'Pasta maken' = to make pasta. 'Gemaakt' = past participle of 'maken'. 'Gekookt' = boiled/cooked, 'gebakken' = fried/baked — possible but 'gemaakt' is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Ik eet eigenlijk altijd ___ de bank.

hint.en: I actually always eat on the sofa.
hint.explanation: 'Op de bank' = on the sofa. Dutch uses 'op' for sitting/eating on furniture surfaces. 'Aan tafel' = at the table (different preposition for table).

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ nu een serie. Murder's Mansion is de titel.

hint.en: I'm currently following a series. Murder's Mansion is the title.
hint.explanation: 'Een serie volgen' = to follow a series (watch it regularly, episode by episode). 'Kijken naar' = to watch. Both exist, but 'volgen' implies regular/ongoing viewing and is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Die ___ je absoluut zien.

hint.en: You absolutely must see that (series).
hint.explanation: 'Moeten' = must (strong recommendation). 'Die moet je zien' = you have to see it. 'Kan' = can (ability); 'wil' = want; 'gaat' = going to — none carry the strong recommendation.

exercises.sentence: Zullen we misschien iets ___ kijken?

hint.en: Shall we perhaps watch something cosy/fun?
hint.explanation: When 'iets' + adjective functions as an indefinite pronoun, Dutch adds -s: 'iets gezelligs', 'iets lekkers', 'iets nieuws'. This is a key A2 grammar pattern.

exercises.sentence: Ik hou niet ___ van series met bloed tijdens het eten.

hint.en: I don't like series with blood so much while eating.
hint.explanation: 'Niet zo van houden' = not to like so much (mild dislike). 'Ik hou niet zo van X' is a softened way of saying 'I don't like X very much'. 'Niet erg van' / 'niet veel van' are less idiomatic.

exercises.sentence: Ik kijk eigenlijk niet ___ tv.

hint.en: I actually don't watch that much TV.
hint.explanation: 'Niet zo veel' = not that much (with 'zo' softening the quantity). 'Niet veel tv kijken' = not watch much TV — both are correct, but 'niet zo veel' is the text's exact phrase and adds a hedging nuance.

exercises.sentence: Soms kijk ik online ___ programma's terug die ik heb gemist.

hint.en: Sometimes I watch a few programmes online that I missed.
hint.explanation: 'Wat programma's' = some/a few programmes. 'Wat' as an indefinite quantity determiner is standard colloquial Dutch. 'Welke' = which (specific selection). 'Sommige' is possible but 'wat' is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Ik kijk eigenlijk ___ programma's van de NPO, niet van andere zenders.

hint.en: I actually mainly watch programmes from NPO, not from other channels.
hint.explanation: 'Vooral' = mainly/above all. It indicates NPO is her primary choice while not excluding everything else. 'Alleen' = only (excludes others entirely — too absolute).

exercises.sentence: Ik heb ook geen ___, om series te kijken.

hint.en: I also don't have a subscription to watch series.
hint.explanation: 'Een abonnement hebben' = to have a subscription (streaming service). 'Geen + singular countable noun'. 'Abonnementen' = plural. 'Abonnering' is not standard Dutch.

exercises.sentence: Er zijn series ___ politiek, drama, comedy's, misdaadseries.

hint.en: There are series about politics, drama, comedies, crime series.
hint.explanation: 'Series over politiek' = series about politics. 'Over' expresses subject matter. 'Van' = of (possession/origin). 'Met' = with (featuring). 'Over' is the preposition for topic/subject.

exercises.sentence: Dat doe ik echt het ___ in het weekend.

hint.en: That's really what I love doing most in the weekend.
hint.explanation: 'Het liefst doen' = to prefer doing most. Bare superlative adverb of 'graag': 'het liefst'. 'Liefste' = attributive adjective. 'Liever' = rather (comparative).

exercises.sentence: Dan kijk ik vaak vier of vijf afleveringen ___ elkaar.

hint.en: Then I often watch four or five episodes in a row.
hint.explanation: 'Achter elkaar' = in a row / consecutively. Fixed Dutch expression. 'Na elkaar' = one after another. 'Voor/bij elkaar' don't fit this meaning.

exercises.sentence: Ik vind het een beetje ___ van mijn tijd om de hele dag series te kijken.

hint.en: I find it a bit of a waste of my time to watch series all day.
hint.explanation: 'Zonde van de tijd' = a waste of time. Fixed Dutch expression. 'Jammer' = a pity (about outcomes, not time). 'Erg' = bad/terrible (intensity).

exercises.sentence: Je ___ even helemaal aan niets te denken.

hint.en: You don't need to think about anything at all for a while.
hint.explanation: 'Niet hoeven te + infinitive' = don't need to. 'Hoeven' is the negative counterpart of 'moeten'. 'Je hoeft niet te denken' is softer/more accurate than 'je moet niet denken'.

exercises.sentence: Echt ___ vind ik dat.

hint.en: I really find that relaxing.
hint.explanation: 'Ontspannen' as predicative adjective = relaxed/relaxing. 'Echt ontspannen vind ik dat' = I really find that relaxing. 'Ontspanning' = relaxation (noun). 'Ontspannend' = relaxing (active participle adjective).

exercises.sentence: Maar wat doe jij dan ___ je vrij bent?

hint.en: But what do you do when you have time off?
hint.explanation: 'Als' = when (conditional/habitual). 'Als je vrij bent' = when you're free. 'Wanneer' is more formal. 'Omdat' = because (reason). 'Terwijl' = while (simultaneous).

exercises.sentence: Of ik ga gewoon even ___ in.

hint.en: Or I just briefly go into town.
hint.explanation: 'De stad ingaan' = to go into town. Directional separable: 'ga de stad in'. The blank is 'de stad'; the particle 'in' is at the end. 'Buiten/weg' change the meaning.

exercises.sentence: Er is ___ ook voetbal op tv.

hint.en: By the way, there's also football on TV.
hint.explanation: 'Trouwens' = by the way / incidentally. Introduces an additional, somewhat unexpected point. 'Namelijk' = you see (explains). 'Bovendien' = moreover (adds a stronger point).

exercises.sentence: Ik ben ___ wie er gaat winnen.

hint.en: I'm curious who is going to win.
hint.explanation: 'Benieuwd zijn' + indirect question (wie/of/wat) = to be curious about. Fixed expression. 'Nieuwsgierig' = curious (personality trait, not followed by 'wie/wat/of'). 'Interessant' = interesting (describes an object).

exercises.sentence: Die ___ echt verslavend.

hint.en: They (those series) are truly addictive.
hint.explanation: 'Die' refers to 'die series' (plural). Plural subject → plural verb: 'zijn'. 'Is' would be for a singular subject. 'Worden' = become (a change, not a current state).

exercises.sentence: Ik heb ___ geen tv.

hint.en: I also don't have a TV.
hint.explanation: 'Ook geen' = also no/not either. Rhana adds that she also lacks a TV, building on her statement about not watching much. 'Al geen' = already no; 'nog geen' = not yet.

exercises.sentence: Een quiz vind ik ___ ook wel leuk.

hint.en: I sometimes find a quiz fun too.
hint.explanation: 'Soms ook wel leuk' = sometimes also quite fun. 'Soms' expresses occasional enjoyment. 'Altijd' = always (too absolute for casual TV preference).

exercises.sentence: Het is niet echt actief ___, of sociaal.

hint.en: It's not really active or anything, or social.
hint.explanation: 'Ofzo' = or something / or whatever. Very common Dutch colloquial hedging word that softens a statement. 'Enzovoorts' = etc. (formal written). 'Enzo' is informal but 'ofzo' is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Ja, ___ is ook leuk natuurlijk.

hint.en: Yes, that is also fun of course.
hint.explanation: 'Dat' = that (anaphoric reference to Rhana's weekend activities mentioned previously). 'Dit' = this (something immediate/present). 'Dat' refers back to what was just said.

exercises.sentence: Ze zijn vorige maand ___.

hint.en: They were born last month.
hint.explanation: 'Geboren zijn' = to be born. Perfect tense: 'ze zijn geboren'. Past participle 'geboren' with auxiliary 'zijn'. 'Geboorte' = birth (noun). 'Geboord' = drilled.

exercises.sentence: We kunnen ze ___ ophalen.

hint.en: We can pick them up for free.
hint.explanation: 'Gratis' = free of charge (adverb). 'Voor gratis' is incorrect Dutch. 'Voor niks' is colloquial and possible. 'Gratis ophalen' = to pick up for free (the text's exact phrase).

exercises.sentence: Ja, dat hebben we toch ___?

hint.en: Yes, we did agree on that, right?
hint.explanation: 'Afspreken' = to agree/arrange. Separable verb: past participle 'afgesproken' (ge- + prefix 'af' + stem 'sproken'). 'Gesproken' = past participle of 'spreken' (to speak) — a different verb.

exercises.sentence: We zijn vorig jaar ___ en we zitten hier nog steeds met z'n tweeën.

hint.en: We moved last year and we're still just the two of us here.
hint.explanation: 'Verhuizen' = to move house. Movement verb → perfect tense with 'zijn': 'zijn verhuisd'. 'Verhuist' = present third person. 'Verhuizen' = infinitive.

exercises.sentence: We zitten hier nog steeds ___ z'n tweeën.

hint.en: We're still just the two of us here.
hint.explanation: 'Met z'n tweeën' = just the two of us. Fixed Dutch expression: 'met z'n + number'. 'Met z'n drieën' = the three of us, etc. 'In/als/van z'n tweeën' are all incorrect.

exercises.sentence: Katten zijn ___ lieve dieren.

hint.en: Cats are incredibly sweet animals.
hint.explanation: 'Hartstikke' = incredibly/really (intensifier, colloquial). 'Hartstikke lief' = incredibly sweet. 'Hartelijk' = heartfelt (a different adjective). 'Heel erg' = very much (used differently).

exercises.sentence: Je ___ ze aaien en knuffelen.

hint.en: You can stroke and cuddle them.
hint.explanation: 'Kunt' = can (ability, second person singular 'je'). Both 'kan' and 'kunt' are accepted for 'je', but 'kunt' is the standard written form. 'Mag' = may (permission).

exercises.sentence: Maar je ___ ze niet de hele dag te verzorgen.

hint.en: But you don't need to look after them all day.
hint.explanation: 'Niet hoeven te + infinitive' = don't need to. 'Hoeven' is the negative counterpart of 'moeten'. 'Je hoeft niet te verzorgen' = you don't need to care for. 'Moet niet' = should not (different meaning).

exercises.sentence: Een hond heeft ___ nadelen.

hint.en: A dog has nothing but disadvantages.
hint.explanation: 'Alleen maar' = nothing but/only (emphatic restriction). 'Een hond heeft alleen maar nadelen' = a dog has only disadvantages. 'Maar alleen' is incorrect word order. 'Slechts' is more formal.

exercises.sentence: Bovendien is ons appartement ___ voor een hond.

hint.en: Moreover, our apartment is too small for a dog.
hint.explanation: 'Te klein' = too small. 'Te + adjective' = too + adjective. 'Klein' alone = just small (without 'too'). 'Te klein voor' = too small for.

exercises.sentence: Een hond ___ ook de hele dag als we niet thuis zijn.

hint.en: A dog also barks all day when we're not home.
hint.explanation: 'Blaffen' = to bark. Third person singular: 'blaft'. 'Blaff' is not a valid Dutch form. 'Klaagt' = complains. 'Bijt' = bites.

exercises.sentence: Een hond ___ niet bij ons.

hint.en: A dog doesn't suit us / doesn't fit our lifestyle.
hint.explanation: 'Passen bij' = to suit/fit with. 'Een hond past niet bij ons' = a dog doesn't suit us. 'Hoort bij' = belongs to/goes with (different nuance). 'Past' specifically expresses suitability.

exercises.sentence: Ze zijn blij als je ___.

hint.en: They are happy when you come home.
hint.explanation: 'Thuiskomen' = to come home. Separable verb. Present tense 'je': 'thuiskomt' (written together, verb at end of 'als' clause). 'Thuis komt' (two words) is an older spelling variant but 'thuiskomt' is standard.

exercises.sentence: Als je je hond uitlaat, maak je veel sneller een ___ met mensen.

hint.en: When you walk your dog, you make conversation with people much faster.
hint.explanation: 'Een praatje maken' = to make small talk / have a chat. Fixed Dutch expression. 'Praatje' = diminutive of 'praat', meaning a little chat. 'Gesprek' = conversation (more formal).

exercises.sentence: Die ___ je niet en ze maken geen rommel.

hint.en: You don't smell them and they don't make a mess.
hint.explanation: 'Ruiken' = to smell. After inversion with 'je': 'die ruik je niet' (the -t drops). Without inversion: 'je ruikt ze niet'. Inversion rule: verb before 'je' → drop -t.

exercises.sentence: Een vis of een vogel komt toch niet ___ je op de bank zitten?

hint.en: A fish or a bird doesn't come and sit next to you on the sofa, does it?
hint.explanation: 'Naast je zitten' = to sit next to you. 'Naast' = next to/beside. 'Bij je zitten' = to sit with/near you (less specific). 'Naast' specifies the physical position beside.

exercises.sentence: We kunnen toch gewoon een keer ___ bij die kleine poesjes?

hint.en: We can just go have a look at those little kittens, right?
hint.explanation: 'Gaan + infinitive' = to go (and do something). After modal 'kunnen': 'we kunnen gaan kijken'. Word order: modal → gaan → infinitive. 'Kijken gaan' is incorrect Dutch word order.

exercises.sentence: Oké, oké. Je hebt me ___.

hint.en: Okay, okay. You've convinced me.
hint.explanation: 'Overtuigen' = to convince. Past participle: 'overtuigd'. Perfect tense: 'je hebt me overtuigd'. 'Overtuigend' = convincing (adjective). 'Overtuigde' = simple past verb form.

exercises.sentence: Maak maar een ___.

hint.en: Just make an appointment.
hint.explanation: 'Een afspraak maken' = to make an appointment/arrangement. Fixed Dutch expression. 'Reservering' = reservation (hotels/restaurants). 'Datum' = date.

exercises.sentence: Dat zijn zulke ___ dieren.

hint.en: Those are such unsociable/dull animals.
hint.explanation: 'Ongezellige dieren' = unsociable animals. Adjective before plural noun: 'ongezellig' + -e. 'Ongezellig' is predicative. 'Zulke + -e adjective' is the standard pattern.

exercises.sentence: Ik wil ___ geen kat.

hint.en: I'd rather not have a cat.
hint.explanation: 'Liever' = rather (comparative of 'graag'). 'Ik wil liever geen kat' = I'd rather not have a cat (preference against). 'Liefst' = most prefer (superlative). 'Liever' expresses preference comparison.

exercises.sentence: Honden zijn ___ heel sociale dieren.

hint.en: Dogs are indeed/do really social animals.
hint.explanation: 'Wel' as a modal particle = indeed/do (concessive/emphatic). Mario concedes that dogs have benefits. 'Honden zijn wel sociale dieren' = dogs are indeed social. 'Toch' = yet/still (also possible but 'wel' is the text's word).

exercises.sentence: Ze zijn zacht en schoon, maar je ___ ze niet de hele dag te verzorgen.

hint.en: They are soft and clean, but you don't need to look after them all day.
hint.explanation: 'Niet hoeven te' = don't need to. 'Hoeft...te' is the formal standard. 'Moet niet' = should not (warns against something). 'Hoeven' specifically means 'don't need to'.

exercises.sentence: Die kan niet goed ___ zijn.

hint.en: That (dog) can't be/be left alone well.
hint.explanation: 'Alleen zijn' = to be alone. 'Niet goed alleen zijn' = to not cope well with being alone. 'Apart' = separately/special. 'Thuis zijn' = to be at home (different meaning).

exercises.sentence: Zo'n beest heeft hier toch ___ ruimte.

hint.en: Such an animal just doesn't have space here.
hint.explanation: 'Geen ruimte hebben' = to have no space. 'Geen' negates the noun 'ruimte'. 'Niet ruimte' is ungrammatical — you cannot use 'niet' to negate a noun directly. 'Weinig' = little (not zero).

exercises.sentence: In het land waar ik ___ kom, heb je veel meer natuur dan in Nederland.

hint.en: In the country where I come from, you have much more nature than in the Netherlands.
hint.explanation: 'Vandaan komen' = to come from (origin). 'Waar ik vandaan kom' = where I come from. 'Vandaan' is the required directional particle. 'Uit komen' means 'to come from' too, but 'waar ik uit kom' is a different (also correct) structure.

exercises.sentence: Het was ___ al een vieze stad met veel verkeer.

hint.en: It was already a dirty city with a lot of traffic back then.
hint.explanation: 'Toen al' = already back then. 'Toen' = then (past reference). 'Dan' = then (logical/conditional). 'Toen al' expresses that the situation existed even at that earlier time.

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ niet van die stad.

hint.en: I didn't like that city.
hint.explanation: 'Houden van' = to like/love. Simple past: 'hield van'. The blog uses past tense throughout for memories. 'Hou' = present tense. 'Vond' = found/thought.

exercises.sentence: In de winter ___ we de sneeuw op de toppen liggen.

hint.en: In winter you could see the snow lying on the peaks.
hint.explanation: Subject is 'je' (general 'you/one'). Past tense of 'zien': 'je zag'. 'Zagen' = we/they saw (plural). The general 'you' takes singular verb.

exercises.sentence: We liepen dan ___ omhoog.

hint.en: We would walk upward for hours.
hint.explanation: 'Urenlang' = for hours (duration adverb, one compound word). Standard written Dutch. 'Uren lang' is an acceptable variant but 'urenlang' (one word) is the established form.

exercises.sentence: Het was ___ om in de natuur te kamperen of vuur te maken.

hint.en: It was allowed to camp in nature or make a fire.
hint.explanation: 'Toegestaan' = permitted/allowed. The blogger contrasts freedoms in their home country with restrictions in Netherlands. 'Verboden' = forbidden (opposite).

exercises.sentence: Eigenlijk hebben mensen ___ alle natuurgebieden in Nederland zelf gemaakt.

hint.en: Actually, people have made almost all nature areas in the Netherlands themselves.
hint.explanation: 'Bijna alle' = almost all. 'Bijna' quantifies/modifies 'alle'. 'Alle' is already in the sentence. 'Haast' = barely/almost (poetic/literary). 'Bijna' is the standard A2 word for 'almost'.

exercises.sentence: De dieren in die gebieden zijn daar ___ niet vanzelf gekomen.

hint.en: The animals in those areas mostly didn't arrive there by themselves.
hint.explanation: 'Meestal' = mostly/in most cases. It softens the statement — not absolutely all, but most animals were introduced. 'Altijd' = always (too absolute). 'Nooit' = never (contradicts 'vanzelf gekomen').

exercises.sentence: Je mag ___ op de paden lopen.

hint.en: You may only walk on the paths.
hint.explanation: 'Alleen...lopen' = only to walk. 'Alleen' as restrictive adverb = only. 'Slechts' is more formal. 'Je mag alleen op de paden lopen' = you may only walk on the paths.

exercises.sentence: Soms moet je ___ betalen, als je in een natuurgebied wil wandelen.

hint.en: Sometimes you even have to pay too, if you want to walk in a nature area.
hint.explanation: 'Ook betalen' = also/too pay. 'Soms moet je ook betalen' = sometimes you have to pay as well. 'Ook' adds this to the list of restrictions already mentioned.

exercises.sentence: Ik vond het ___, al die verschillende kleuren.

hint.en: I found it beautiful, all those different colours.
hint.explanation: 'Prachtig' = wonderful/beautiful (predicative after 'vinden'). No -e ending. 'Prachtige' would be attributive (before a noun). 'Mooi' = beautiful (also correct but 'prachtig' is the text's word).

exercises.sentence: ___ hoorde ik dat dit geen origineel bos is.

hint.en: Later I heard that this is not an original forest.
hint.explanation: 'Later' = later (subsequent discovery, some time after). 'Daarna' = after that (implies immediate sequence). 'Toen' = then (immediate past). 'Na' is a preposition requiring an object.

exercises.sentence: Mensen hebben een groot deel van het bos ___ gemaakt.

hint.en: People have made a large part of the forest that way.
hint.explanation: 'Zo gemaakt' = made that way/like that. 'Zo' refers back to the managed/artificial way described. 'Zelf gemaakt' = made themselves (changes the meaning — it's about the method, not the agent).

exercises.sentence: Toen ___ ik wel teleurgesteld.

hint.en: I was quite disappointed then.
hint.explanation: 'Was' = simple past of 'zijn' (to be). 'Teleurgesteld zijn' = to be disappointed (state). 'Werd teleurgesteld' = was disappointed (passive process). 'Was' describes the resulting feeling.

exercises.sentence: Het mooiste stuk natuur in Nederland ___ ik de stranden.

hint.en: I find the beaches the most beautiful stretch of nature in the Netherlands.
hint.explanation: Fronted object 'het mooiste stuk' causes inversion: 'vind ik'. The blank is 'vind'; 'ik' is the subject after inversion. 'Denk' = think; 'houd' = hold/keep.

exercises.sentence: Als het stormt, ___ het zand overal naartoe.

hint.en: When it storms, the sand blows everywhere.
hint.explanation: 'Waaien' = to blow (wind). 'Het zand waait' = the sand blows. 'Vliegt' = flies (birds/objects, not sand in wind). 'Waaien' is the weather verb for wind movement.

exercises.sentence: Gelukkig ___ de overheid de kust in Nederland goed.

hint.en: Fortunately the government protects the Dutch coastline well.
hint.explanation: 'Beschermen' = to protect. Singular subject 'de overheid' → 'beschermt'. 'Beveiligen' = to secure (usually buildings/security). 'Bewaren' = to preserve/keep/store.

exercises.sentence: Het is niet ___ om daar zomaar hotels of huizen te bouwen.

hint.en: It is not permitted to just build hotels or houses there.
hint.explanation: 'Niet toegestaan' = not permitted. 'Het is niet toegestaan om' + infinitive = it is not allowed to. 'Verboden' = forbidden (stronger legal term; also possible but 'toegestaan' is the text's word).

exercises.sentence: Overal langs de kust ___ hoge flats en hotels.

hint.en: Everywhere along the coast there are tall apartment blocks and hotels.
hint.explanation: 'Staan' = to stand (for buildings and vertical structures). 'Langs de kust staan flats'. 'Liggen' = to lie (horizontal objects). 'Zijn' is possible but 'staan' is the geographical standard for buildings.

exercises.sentence: De natuur is daar ___ en komt niet meer terug.

hint.en: Nature has disappeared there and won't come back.
hint.explanation: 'Verdwenen zijn' = to have disappeared. Predicative past participle. 'Weg zijn' = to be gone (colloquial). 'Verdwenen' is more literary and matches the blog's tone.

exercises.sentence: Nederland is een ___ en groen land.

hint.en: The Netherlands is a beautiful and green country.
hint.explanation: 'Mooi' before neuter noun 'land' with indefinite article 'een': adjective stays uninflected: 'een mooi land'. 'Mooie' would be for de-words or definite article. (Note: 'mooi en groen land' — both adjectives are uninflected).

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ me thuis in de natuur.

hint.en: I felt at home in nature.
hint.explanation: 'Zich thuis voelen' = to feel at home. Simple past: 'voelde me'. The blog narrates past memories. 'Voel' = present tense. 'Gevoeld' = past participle.

exercises.sentence: Om die natuurgebieden ___ een hek heen.

hint.en: Around those nature areas there is a fence.
hint.explanation: 'Staan' = to stand (for fences). 'Er staat een hek' = there is a fence. 'Om X heen staan' = to stand around X. Singular 'een hek' → 'staat'.

exercises.sentence: Maar het landschap is erg ___, vind ik.

hint.en: But the landscape is very flat, I think.
hint.explanation: 'Plat' = flat (landscape). 'Vlak' = flat (surface, also used for landscape but 'plat' is the text's word). 'Laag' = low (elevation). 'Klein' = small.

exercises.sentence: Ik hou van de groene weilanden met koeien, schapen en ___.

hint.en: I like the green meadows with cows, sheep and farms.
hint.explanation: 'Boerderijen' = farms (plural of 'boerderij'). The list 'koeien, schapen en boerderijen' = cows, sheep and farms. All three are in plural. 'Boeren' = farmers (people).

exercises.sentence: Het weer in Nederland kan iedere dag ___ zijn.

hint.en: The weather in the Netherlands can be different every day.
hint.explanation: 'Anders' = different (predicative adverb/adjective after 'zijn'). No -e ending after 'zijn'. 'Andere' = attributive (before a noun, e.g., 'andere dag'). 'Verscheiden' = various.

exercises.sentence: Het Nederlandse klimaat heeft ___ winters en milde zomers.

hint.en: The Dutch climate has mild winters and mild summers.
hint.explanation: 'Milde winters' = mild winters. Adjective before de-word plural: 'mild' + -e = 'milde'. The text explicitly uses 'milde winters en milde zomers'. Factual recall + grammar check.

exercises.sentence: Veel mensen hebben het ___ dat Nederland een erg nat klimaat heeft.

hint.en: Many people have the feeling that the Netherlands has a very wet climate.
hint.explanation: 'Het gevoel hebben dat' = to have the feeling that. Fixed expression. 'Het gevoel' = subjective impression. 'Idee' = idea (more cognitive). 'Beeld' = image.

exercises.sentence: Maar dit is een ___.

hint.en: But this is a misconception.
hint.explanation: 'Misverstand' = misconception/misunderstanding. The text calls the 'wet Netherlands' belief a 'misverstand'. 'Vergissing' = mistake (personal error). 'Fout' = error. 'Leugen' = lie.

exercises.sentence: De Nederlandse zomers zijn vaak ___.

hint.en: Dutch summers are often unpredictable/changeable.
hint.explanation: 'Wisselvallig' = changeable/unpredictable (specifically for weather). 'Wisselend' = varying. 'Wisselvallig' is the specific weather term used in the text.

exercises.sentence: Als je op vakantie in Nederland gaat, moet je altijd ___ met slecht weer houden.

hint.en: If you go on holiday in the Netherlands, you must always take bad weather into account.
hint.explanation: 'Rekening houden met' = to take into account/consider. Fixed Dutch idiom. Cannot be replaced by other nouns — 'rekening' is the required word in this expression.

exercises.sentence: Je hebt de ___ kans op mooi weer aan de kust.

hint.en: You have the most chance of nice weather at the coast.
hint.explanation: 'De meeste kans' = the most chance/likelihood. 'De meeste + noun' = most of the. 'De grootste kans' is also commonly used but 'meeste' is the text's exact word.

exercises.sentence: ___ de auto of het vliegtuig naar het zuiden of het oosten van Europa.

hint.en: Take the car or the plane to the south or east of Europe.
hint.explanation: 'Neem' = take (imperative of 'nemen'). 'De auto/het vliegtuig nemen' = to take the car/plane. 'Pak' = grab/take (more informal, often for smaller items). 'Neem' is standard for transport.

exercises.sentence: De winters zijn de laatste vijftig jaar ___ minder koud geworden.

hint.en: The winters have indeed become less cold over the last fifty years.
hint.explanation: 'Wel' as modal particle = indeed/do (concessive/emphatic). Acknowledges a real but perhaps surprising trend. 'Echt' = really; 'zeker' = certainly — both possible but 'wel' is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Vroeger kon je in Nederland ___ bijna iedere winter schaatsen.

hint.en: For example, in the past you could skate in the Netherlands almost every winter.
hint.explanation: 'Bijvoorbeeld' = for example. The text uses it to give skating as an example of the warmer winters' effect. 'Misschien' = maybe (introduces uncertainty that doesn't fit here).

exercises.sentence: Tegenwoordig is ___ een uitzondering.

hint.en: Nowadays that is an exception.
hint.explanation: 'Dat' = that (anaphoric reference to skating every winter, mentioned in the previous sentence). 'Dit' = this (immediate/nearby reference). 'Dat' refers back to what was just said.

exercises.sentence: De sneeuw ___ ook minder lang liggen.

hint.en: The snow also stays on the ground for less time.
hint.explanation: 'Blijven liggen' = to remain lying (on the ground). 'Sneeuw blijft liggen' = snow stays. 'Valt' = falls (snow falling, not staying). 'Legt' = lays (transitive, wrong use).

exercises.sentence: In Nederland ___ we dus ook dat het klimaat verandert.

hint.en: In the Netherlands we thus also notice that the climate is changing.
hint.explanation: 'Merken' = to notice/perceive. 'We merken dat' = we notice/are experiencing that. 'Zien dat' = to see that (more visual). 'Merken' implies experiencing a change firsthand.

exercises.sentence: De aarde wordt ___ warmer.

hint.en: The earth is getting warmer and warmer.
hint.explanation: 'Steeds warmer' = warmer and warmer / increasingly warm. 'Steeds + comparative' = progressively more. 'Altijd' = always (a fixed state, not progressive). 'Meer warmer' is ungrammatical Dutch.

exercises.sentence: ___ krijgen we minder koude winters en hetere, drogere zomers.

hint.en: As a result of that, we get less cold winters and hotter, drier summers.
hint.explanation: 'Daardoor' = because of that/as a result (refers back to the warming earth). 'Hierdoor' = because of this (more immediate). 'Daarvoor' = for that/before that. 'Daardoor' = consequence of something previously mentioned.

exercises.sentence: De hogere temperaturen hebben ook ___ op de neerslag.

hint.en: The higher temperatures also have an influence on the precipitation.
hint.explanation: 'Invloed hebben op' = to have an influence on. Fixed Dutch collocation. 'Effect op' is also possible but 'invloed hebben op' is the standard and is the text's phrase.

exercises.sentence: Het regent en stormt ___ in de winter en de lente vaker.

hint.en: It rains and storms more often especially in winter and spring.
hint.explanation: 'Vooral' = especially/particularly. It highlights which seasons see more rain. 'Vaker' is already in the sentence as the comparative. 'Altijd' = always (too absolute).

exercises.sentence: In de zomer ___ en stormt het veel minder.

hint.en: In summer it rains and storms much less.
hint.explanation: The text: 'In de zomer regent en stormt het veel minder'. 'Regent' = rains. The structure links '[regent] en stormt het' — both weather verbs. Context: summer has less rain and storms.

exercises.sentence: Het westen van Nederland ___ voor het grootste deel lager dan de Noordzee.

hint.en: The west of the Netherlands lies largely below the North Sea.
hint.explanation: 'Liggen' = to lie/be located (geographical position). 'Lager liggen dan'. 'Staan' is for vertical structures. 'Liggen' is the geographic standard for land/regions.

exercises.sentence: Als het water ___, hebben we meer kans op wateroverlast.

hint.en: If the water rises, we have more chance of flooding.
hint.explanation: 'Stijgen' = to rise (water level, prices, etc.). 'Het water stijgt' = the water rises. 'Groeien' = to grow (organisms). 'Stromen' = to flow. 'Vallen' = to fall.

exercises.sentence: Verschillende dammen en dijken moeten Nederland ___ dit hoge water beschermen.

hint.en: Various dams and dikes must protect the Netherlands against this high water.
hint.explanation: 'Beschermen tegen' = to protect against. Fixed prepositional collocation. 'Beschermen voor' is incorrect Dutch. 'Tegen' is the standard preposition with 'beschermen'.

exercises.sentence: Op sommige dagen ___ het bloed heet, de zon schijnt en de lucht is blauw.

hint.en: On some days it is boiling hot, the sun shines and the sky is blue.
hint.explanation: 'Het is bloed heet' = it is boiling hot. Present tense of 'zijn': 'is'. 'Wordt' = becomes (a change). 'Is' describes a current state of the weather on those days.

exercises.sentence: Klimaatverandering kan grote ___ voor Nederland hebben.

hint.en: Climate change can have major consequences for the Netherlands.
hint.explanation: 'Grote gevolgen hebben' = to have major consequences. 'Gevolgen' = consequences (plural of 'gevolg'). 'Grote gevolgen' — the plural is needed here. 'Gevolg' (singular) doesn't match the plural adjective 'grote'.

exercises.sentence: Er zijn ook vaak periodes met regen en ___.

hint.en: There are also often periods with rain and thunderstorms.
hint.explanation: 'Onweer' = thunderstorm. 'Regen en onweer' = rain and thunderstorms. The text's exact word. 'Bliksem' = lightning (part of thunderstorm). 'Storm' = storm (different intensity).

exercises.sentence: Het weer in de winter in Nederland is ___ te voorspellen.

hint.en: The weather in winter in the Netherlands is difficult to predict.
hint.explanation: 'Moeilijk te voorspellen' = difficult to predict. 'Moeilijk + te + infinitive' is a standard Dutch construction. 'Makkelijk' = easy (opposite). 'Niet te voorspellen' = impossible to predict (too strong).

exercises.sentence: Dit is echt geen weer ___ te fietsen.

hint.en: This is really no weather to cycle in.
hint.explanation: 'Om te + infinitive' expresses suitability/purpose: 'geen weer om te fietsen' = no weather to cycle in. Standard Dutch infinitive complement construction after 'goed/slecht/geen X'.

exercises.sentence: Heb je vannacht die storm ___ gehoord?

hint.en: Did you also hear that storm last night?
hint.explanation: 'Ook' = also/too. Barbara asks if Jaïr also heard what she heard (shared experience). 'Al' = already; 'nog' = still; 'wel' = indeed — 'ook' best fits the inclusive shared-experience question.

exercises.sentence: Het waaide zo hard en het ___ ook.

hint.en: It blew so hard and there was also thunder and lightning.
hint.explanation: 'Onweren' = to thunder/have a thunderstorm. Simple past: 'onweerde'. The text specifically says 'onweerde'. 'Regende' = rained; 'stormde' = stormed — both possible but 'onweerde' is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ precies in die bui, toen ik naar huis ging.

hint.en: I cycled right into that rain shower when I went home.
hint.explanation: 'Fietste' = simple past of 'fietsen'. Narrative past tense for ongoing action alongside another past event ('toen ik naar huis ging'). 'Fiets' = present tense.

exercises.sentence: Ze ___ voor vanmiddag ook hagel.

hint.en: They are forecasting hail for this afternoon too.
hint.explanation: 'Voorspellen' = to forecast/predict. 'Ze voorspellen hagel' = they forecast hail. The weather-specific verb. 'Verwachten' = expect; 'zeggen' = say — less specific for weather forecasting.

exercises.sentence: Ik heb zo'n ___ aan die harde wind.

hint.en: I have such a dislike of that strong wind.
hint.explanation: 'Een hekel hebben aan' = to dislike/hate something. Fixed Dutch expression. 'Haat' = hatred (too strong, different register). 'Afkeer' = aversion (formal). 'Angst' = fear.

exercises.sentence: Je haar en je kleren ___ helemaal nat.

hint.en: Your hair and clothes get completely wet.
hint.explanation: 'Worden' = to become/get. 'Helemaal nat worden' = to get completely wet (change of state). 'Zijn' = are (already wet — static state). 'Worden' captures the process of becoming.

exercises.sentence: Dan ___ ik zo chagrijnig.

hint.en: Then I become so grumpy.
hint.explanation: 'Worden' = to become. First person singular: 'ik word' (not 'wordt' which is hij/zij/het). Change of state: 'chagrijnig worden'. 'Ben' = am (static state).

exercises.sentence: Je kleren ___ niet zo snel.

hint.en: Your clothes don't dry that quickly.
hint.explanation: 'Drogen' = to dry (intransitive). Plural subject 'kleren' → 'drogen'. 'Droogt' is singular. 'Drogen op' = to dry up (separable, slightly different meaning).

exercises.sentence: Gelukkig ___ we met het ov.

hint.en: Luckily we can use public transport.
hint.explanation: 'Met het ov kunnen' = to be able to use public transport. Ability/option: 'kunnen'. 'Mogen' = may (permission); 'moeten' = must (obligation); 'willen' = want.

exercises.sentence: En ___ we droog op kantoor aan.

hint.en: And we arrive at the office dry.
hint.explanation: 'Aankomen op kantoor' = to arrive at the office. Separable verb: 'komen...aan'. The text says 'komen we droog...aan'. 'Gaan' = go. 'Arriveren' = arrive (formal, not used with 'aan').

exercises.sentence: De wedstrijd ___ vast niet door.

hint.en: The match surely won't go ahead.
hint.explanation: 'Doorgaan' = to continue/go ahead. Separable: 'gaat...door'. Singular 'de wedstrijd' → 'gaat'. 'Doet door' / 'loopt door' are wrong uses of the separable verb.

exercises.sentence: Het is ___ weer om buiten te sporten.

hint.en: It's excellent weather to sport outside.
hint.explanation: 'Prima weer' = excellent/fine weather. The text uses 'prima'. 'Goed' = good; 'leuk' = fun; 'fijn' = nice — all possible, but 'prima' is the text's exact word and a key A2 adjective.

exercises.sentence: Kijk ___ naar de grijze wolken buiten.

hint.en: Just look at the grey clouds outside.
hint.explanation: 'Kijk dan' = just look (then). 'Dan' as a discourse particle intensifies the imperative (= come on, look!). 'Maar eens kijken' is another common pattern. The text uses 'dan'.

exercises.sentence: Je gaat echt niet ___ van wat regen.

hint.en: You're really not going to die from a bit of rain.
hint.explanation: 'Doodgaan van' = to die from. Ivan uses 'je gaat niet dood van wat regen' as a hyperbolic reassurance (= it won't kill you). Very common Dutch expression.

exercises.sentence: Trek gewoon een lange broek ___ en een paar handschoenen.

hint.en: Just put on some long trousers and a pair of gloves.
hint.explanation: 'Aantrekken' = to put on (clothing). Separable: 'trek...aan'. 'Uittrekken' = to take off. 'Omdoen' = for scarves/accessories. 'Opzetten' = for hats.

exercises.sentence: ___ een muts op je hoofd.

hint.en: Put a hat on your head.
hint.explanation: 'Opzetten' = to put on (a hat, glasses). Separable imperative: 'Zet...op'. 'Doe op' = for lids/covers. 'Trek aan' = for clothes. 'Zet op' is the standard for hats.

exercises.sentence: In de app staat dat de zon vanmiddag ___ schijnen.

hint.en: The app says the sun is going to shine this afternoon.
hint.explanation: 'Gaan + infinitive' = near future (based on evidence/information). 'De zon gaat schijnen' = the sun is going to shine. 'Zal' is future too but more formal/hypothetical.

exercises.sentence: Niet zo ___ dus en schiet op, want je komt te laat.

hint.en: So stop whining and hurry up, you'll be late.
hint.explanation: 'Zeuren' = to whine/nag (about trivial things). Ivan says 'niet zo zeuren' = stop whining. 'Klagen' = to complain (more serious). 'Mopperen' = to grumble. 'Zeuren' is the text's word.

exercises.sentence: Ik kan niet ___ tot het écht zomer is.

hint.en: I can't wait until it's really summer.
hint.explanation: 'Niet kunnen wachten' = can't wait. 'Kan niet wachten tot' = can't wait until. 'Wachten' = infinitive after modal 'kan'. 'Wachten op' = to wait for (something/someone).

exercises.sentence: Ik vond het een beetje ___.

hint.en: I found it a bit scary.
hint.explanation: 'Eng' = scary (predicative after 'vinden'). No -e ending. 'Enge' is attributive. 'Bang' = afraid (a person's feeling). 'Engst' = scariest (superlative).

exercises.sentence: Hé, ___ je ook met de tram vandaag?

hint.en: Hey, are you also taking the tram today?
hint.explanation: 'Met de tram zijn' = to be (going) by tram. After inversion with 'je', the -t drops: 'ben je' (not 'bent je'). This is the inversion rule for 'jij/je'.

exercises.sentence: Het is ___ buiten en het gaat zo regenen.

hint.en: It's ice cold outside and it's about to rain.
hint.explanation: 'IJskoud' = ice cold. Written as one compound word. 'Ijs koud' (two words) is a spelling error. 'Heel koud' = very cold — 'ijskoud' is a stronger intensifier compound.

exercises.sentence: Ik wilde ___ het zomer was, lekker 25 graden.

hint.en: I wish it were summer, a lovely 25 degrees.
hint.explanation: 'Ik wilde dat' + past tense = I wish (that)... Expresses a counterfactual wish. 'Ik wilde dat het zomer was' = I wish it were summer. Fixed wish construction in Dutch.

exercises.sentence: Met die ___ moet je niet sporten.

hint.en: In that heat you shouldn't sport.
hint.explanation: 'Hitte' = heat (intense, uncomfortable). 'Met die hitte' = in that heat. 'Warmte' = warmth (pleasant). 'Hitte' specifically refers to unpleasant, oppressive heat — Ivan uses it to argue against 25 degrees.
